mnemonics: DADS Can Really Understand Systems (DCRUS): Diagram, Activity, Diagram, Sequence, Use Case, State Machine
keyPoints: Use Case Diagrams are the starting point, but other UML models provide detailed system behavior.
Activity Diagrams model the flow of control between activities and are useful for business process modeling.
Sequence Diagrams show object interactions arranged in time sequence, focusing on the order of messages.
commonMistakes: Confusing Activity Diagrams (flow of activities) with Sequence Diagrams (interaction between objects over time).
Creating overly complex diagrams that try to show everything instead of a single, clear aspect of the system.
